name: ServiceConfigurationChange (Sysmon config change)
definition: Sysmon Event ID 16 records changes to the active Sysmon configuration.
trigger_scenarios: Sysmon emits this event when the service configuration changes, including when filtering rules are updated. Microsoft documents Sysmon configuration entries such as ArchiveDirectory, HashAlgorithms, DnsLookup, CopyOnDeletePE, and event-filtering include/exclude rules.

key_fields.explanation: Review the recorded configuration change against the approved Sysmon XML baseline. Changes to HashAlgorithms, ArchiveDirectory, CopyOnDeletePE, or event filters can materially change telemetry coverage.

Microsoft documents include/exclude filter tags such as ProcessCreate, NetworkConnect, DriverLoad, ImageLoad, PipeEvent, and RegistryEvent. Removing coverage or adding broad exclusions can blind detection.
Use the configuration-change time to correlate with service-control events, file changes, and process creation from administrators or deployment tools.

false_positives: Approved administration, management agents, and deployment tools can generate this telemetry.
Baseline expected hosts, signed binaries, and change windows before suppression.
detection_notes: T1562.001 Disable or Modify Tools is supported when Event 16 coincides with sysmon64.exe -c C:\Windows\config.xml and the approved XML diff removes ProcessCreate, NetworkConnect, DriverLoad, ImageLoad, PipeEvent, or RegistryEvent coverage, or changes HashAlgorithms from SHA256 or * to weaker coverage. Event 16 establishes that configuration changed but does not by itself prove which filter changed; compare the deployed file with the controlled baseline and validate UtcTime against process creation and the change ticket.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 16
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet: index=sysmon EventCode=16 | table _time, host, Image, ProcessGuid
sample_log:
UtcTime: 2026-07-13 01:00:00.000
Configuration: EventFiltering updated; ProcessCreate onmatch=exclude
HashAlgorithms: SHA256
ArchiveDirectory: Sysmon

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 16
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 16
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 16
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=16
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the event-specific field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 16
assumptions: Confirm Provider is Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 16
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 16
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 16
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the documented field conditions and correlations in the destination SIEM.

value_references.meaning: The active Sysmon configuration changed; this event cannot be filtered out.
value_references.security_relevance: Use EventID together with the other documented event fields and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The documented wildcard enables all supported hashing algorithms.
value_references.security_relevance: Use HashAlgorithms together with the other documented event fields and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 16 mean?
faqs.answer: The active Sysmon configuration changed, such as an update to event filtering.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 16 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Approved configuration deployment and tuning generate it legitimately.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 16 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Compare the new configuration with the approved XML baseline and correlate the time with sysmon64.exe process and change records.
